reasoning_model_1: The model generates the audio with perfect content accuracy, matching the script word-for-word. In terms of fluency and naturalness, the voice is human-like and smooth. However, regarding the core paralinguistic instruction 'fast pace', the model fails to adhere to the prompt. The speech is delivered at a moderate, conversational speed (taking approximately 12 seconds to complete the text). There is no noticeable acceleration in the rhythm or tempo to characterize a 'fast pace'; in fact, the delivery feels somewhat relaxed. While the tone is pleasant, it completely misses the specific speed constraint requested by the user.
reasoning_model_2: The model generates the audio with perfect content accuracy and high fluency. The articulation is crisp, and the prosody is natural. Regarding the core paralinguistic instruction 'fast pace', this model performs excellent work. The delivery is noticeably accelerated (taking approximately 8 seconds to complete the text), creating a sense of excitement and urgency that fits the semantic content well. Despite the faster speed, the enunciation remains clear and intelligible. The model successfully integrates the high energy required by the text with the specific 'fast' tempo constraint.
model_comparison: Both models achieve perfect content accuracy and high naturalness in terms of voice quality. The decisive difference lies in the adherence to the specific 'fast pace' instruction. Model 1 (T1) reads the text at a standard, moderate speed, failing to implement the requested feature. Model 2 (T2) successfully increases the speaking rate, delivering the text significantly faster than Model 1 while maintaining clarity and energy. T2 effectively captures the 'fast pace' characteristic, whereas T1 ignores it.
